I was born and raised here. I graduated from Douglas County public schools and earned a full scholarship to attend college. And when both my parents got sick with cancer at the same time, I came home to take care of them.
This community raised me. I will never abandon it.
Boebert pretends to be a down-to-earth Coloradan. But she’s a carpetbagger funded by billionaires like Elon Musk, more interested in cable news hits than kitchen table issues.
We can flip this seat – with all hands on deck. So please: Will you chip in $20, $10, $5 or anything you can to help us bring real representation back to Washington? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/trisha-dsg-email?refcode=260221em-rs]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/trisha-dsg-email?refcode=260221em-rs]
Colorado families deserve someone who listens.
I promise to show up.
— Trisha

name: Camille Alcala
subject: texting you from the floor
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www
body:
here’s what keeps running through my head: |

---
| Hi there - I’m on the Convention floor right now and I wish you could feel this energy. We’ve heard speech after speech about what’s at stake in November and what we just proved by working together to pass Prop 50. It’s powerful beyond belief. But here’s what keeps running through my head: The moment Convention ends, the real work begins again. The path to taking back the House runs through California. Proposition 50 means we have fair maps to keep the possibility of success alive - but we have to do the work to flip key seats and protect our vulnerable incumbents. It’ll require nonstop organizing for us to retake the U.S. House, and we have to ramp up our efforts the very minute the Convention gavels out. That’s why I’m concerned we fell ~$1K short of our pre-Convention goal. If you’re feeling fired up at this moment, chip in $20.26 to help us close the gap and carry this energy straight into the fight to flip seats: **[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-feb26](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/125871438/603175832/398823957?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkODRkOWU2NS04MjBmLWYxMTEtYTY5YS0wMDBkM2E1NzU5M2YiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7vMpKfPgXDcrgXjRqRBsaKrk6ZJ5IpWxe_tEl6YOROY=)** Back to the floor -- Camille

clean_body: here’s what keeps running through my head: | | Hi there - I’m on the Convention floor right now and I wish you could feel this energy. We’ve heard speech after speech about what’s at stake in November and what we just proved by working together to pass Prop 50. It’s powerful beyond belief. But here’s what keeps running through my head: The moment Convention ends, the real work begins again. The path to taking back the House runs through California. Proposition 50 means we have fair maps to keep the possibility of success alive - but we have to do the work to flip key seats and protect our vulnerable incumbents. It’ll require nonstop organizing for us to retake the U.S. House, and we have to ramp up our efforts the very minute the Convention gavels out. That’s why I’m concerned we fell ~$1K short of our pre-Convention goal. If you’re feeling fired up at this moment, chip in $20.26 to help us close the gap and carry this energy straight into the fight to flip seats: = Back to the floor -- Camille | | = Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. name: DNC HQ
subject: 60 seconds — that’s it:
body:
---
Hi Derek, We’ll keep this brief: In less than 60 seconds, we’re going to ask you to make a donation to the DNC. [](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-february-2026?refcode=em_20260221_6166_fr&amount=7&refcode2=6166_22930333_eyciVF&akid=6166%2E22930333%2EeyciVF) Not tomorrow, not next week, but _today._ Here’s the deal: The midterms are approaching quickly. The _only_ way we can bring Trump’s agenda to a grinding halt is by taking back Congress. But, to do it, we’ll need a huge sum of resources to scale up our voter outreach efforts. Your contribution today will help us:
* Register thousands of Democratic voters across the country.
* Support local and state parties by moving resources where they’re needed, and when they’re needed most.

[**The earlier we’re able to make these strategic investments, the better our odds of reclaiming Congress this year. Chip in whatever is meaningful to you to join the thousands of other Democrats chipping in today:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-february-2026?amount=7&refcode=em_20260221_6166_fr&refcode2=6166_22930333_eyciVF&akid=6166%2E22930333%2EeyciVF) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-february-2026?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20260221_6166_fr&refcode2=6166_22930333_eyciVF&akid=6166%2E22930333%2EeyciVF)**

body:
I will have to move to the next person if you don’t claim your spot.
Fellow American,
If one thing is true, it's that you have stood with House Republicans. You've supported the conservative fight furiously and consistently through every battle we've seen, BUT we have bad news for you.
[It pains me to tell you that if you do NOT claim your Freedom 250 Membership before midnight, I will have to move on and give YOUR SPOT away.](http://links.conservativesdefendingfreedom.com/u/click?_t=91d01aa68bbe4105984439520ca0f0af&_m=08ec2ebd40f74d029d1e90a46fce1924&_e=W0ekJo_KQyZyGcu4p7rMLiuc3Wz1DqpvqqQLug_3O70Movr3nuGbRtRAtqnsRVTl3b4BoMk4lfofJjHm5roTT2Kqky_ThCVbBbPFL_QrSu6SXzZ67QSiMofygkC-VsHcsXNl6BHAcRR74piJmc0Od8pr4fCPAYzmEZ5X-r0hIKeARrq4CuR_idpCCEa9Fl4aXjknY5UwJRcb2-e5ADlyRdqPLvXsLBicPKABbLO24iv4aCezh9uvJ_5lIfH_4dHEImGl-hl9idSuS2XZljLwpt2a7I0WIJLK4rfsN_tirOqdF-ADWc4o8YQjTEp9rokxCgK-BOuQNbHT_kJeaF4JDCv-NSV8-3w1phUMOtf6QFVacQoNW-OrtmC4p8SF7z3IrO6hpb0rqY0LWmOXWgx19nrSYFmWHbHgB_RRZo0hBLTSOAlVAW5QI93bSzPBVGUaSUw-cCQkfdEMc6HsBEGxNmYiIeBiJfz5kc49fnl5fzvlDOVBhuecPd0uuuup22uLBliQgSEi_vqJQI4wzfGSLar2tSHqVTMRh64gY1rk4g358pDrypNYo0MCfPP8IGn6PhTWL50I2mNUGYqUNHELFYq_X006_I6hSGv_FBpquD4JrEIZ6Ad4cl_hKB_DNEdawtqH1FgbyYeIaDfmZiqtlorrnFRabo3bU2H9pr3Jgjnk6YvBAypQsjCvAXgX2CUufSBHFTQ0iIpu3Ut3FUHkghHX0CpwD-VAO2Hg8dcYVGFJWsDU-4midP0fTO9lRxaxLETkP3RVtaKSMdOXbjJwqcAi13tEVmEpaMCYfsz_L8SUsweAnX_X3OuflehuU3d7kIbb1HEcm74tCkdDFMH3ZMWb5CI9HI-q4N6DUfoBm8c%3D)

You've supported the conservative fight furiously and consistently through every battle we've seen, BUT we have bad news for you. There's good news though, Fellow American. I spoke with leadership about you, and they gave me ONE FINAL CHANCE to extend our deadline to are about to enter the biggest battle of our lives. The 2026 Elections will take everything we have - the fight is far from over. More than ever, we need our strongest grassroots supporters by our side. You only have until to let us know if you plan to stand against the Left and their radical socialist agenda and STAND for 250 years of America. name: John James (Combat Vet)
subject: I saw my life flash before my eyes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by John James for MI, 35744 Van Dyke Avenue, Sterling Heights, MI 48312
body:
| | | Friend, Faith kept me strong. When I was serving in Iraq, there were moments where my life flashed before my eyes.

I was in the sky in a helicopter being shot at, and we could go down at any time.

_**In that moment, I leaned on my faith in Christ more than ever.**_

My Christian faith got me through the toughest moments serving in Iraq. Even when my life was on the line, my faith kept me strong.

Trusting in something bigger than me has been pivotal in my life, and that’s why I’m so passionate about standing up for religious freedom for all.

Right now, the woke Left is trampling on our Christian values by forcing us to accept things that we know go against our beliefs.

**I won't stand by it.**

I’m calling on you to join me in defending religious freedom for EVERYONE to be able to live according to their faith without politicians intervening.
---
[ **JOIN MY FIGHT** ](http://links.jjformichigan.com/u/click?_t=70fde66bc0554ca7a14c16f5ebafdbd9&_m=6b9301e81806456889197ca4516b307e&_e=0U6Wy3D4WnuJ3ED4rHKwCXH6XHUf2pHX0gvVMybpIT4CEUiL8RO0rlSrnC3hGIq8lvG9N4bP4o-01WlJExgF4cUONo1s0FGKKcwCuy4MRlYGGzjIe-vzFCxd1NXSkiYA-XdQQ4EyxtUYaL4OJSAgqvfUSU-CnbduTZlOrEbS4MOjflu3HlbwkqL97vdvVLFaSuRrt3MixFG5StN2tDdVMSfeXkwUA02ITAW_TT0BlvtttS2_o2MzHb_PrRr6fWhb7hnz34zpOdqoF4Q03Xs8-Lkz0ABGHk5FDxXnZUyvAtRjGXl-HR1df7Tl8fWJM29b5xN3tu_1IsCXh0zU_-HGFwQzLmKgqnuYLrAG04spf_7MT_DdM56NuoP0vJW6IfMEreLqUgsPAuDOVpzMroCcz7EgFKLNpoD2NmUCxfhdx5Q%3D)

clean_body: | | | Friend, Faith kept me strong. When I was serving in Iraq, there were moments where my life flashed before my eyes. I was in the sky in a helicopter being shot at, and we could go down at any time. In that moment, I leaned on my faith in Christ more than ever. My Christian faith got me through the toughest moments serving in Iraq. Even when my life was on the line, my faith kept me strong. Trusting in something bigger than me has been pivotal in my life, and that’s why I’m so passionate about standing up for religious freedom for all. Right now, the woke Left is trampling on our Christian values by forcing us to accept things that we know go against our beliefs. I won't stand by it. I’m calling on you to join me in defending religious freedom for EVERYONE to be able to live according to their faith without politicians intervening. I’m raising my family with Christian values. It’s important to me that my children find the same comfort in faith that my wife and I have. But in educational institutions across the country, the woke mob is trying to take away that right and force their Godless, Marxist propaganda on our children. Let me be clear: FAMILIES should decide how to raise our children, not politicians or school boards. I will ALWAYS stand for religious freedom and our right to raise our children with Christian values. Thank you,John James | | Paid for by John James for MI, 35744 Van Dyke Avenue, Sterling Heights, MI 48312 | | Email sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com by John James for MI Use of John James’ military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Army or the Department of Defense.

| | | | | | High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.

Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.

At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts.

By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch. She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground. Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina.

Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks.

She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.

Nancy is fighting to:

* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast

* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency

* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free

* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate

* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think

* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front.

South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.

As Mayor of Boca Raton, Scott's tax-cutting agenda led to an **EXPLOSION** of economic growth.

As Chair of the America First Policy Institute Mayors’ Council, he led a team of Republican mayors to **crack down on crime and secure our elections.**

_**In other words, Scott is proof that Zohran Mamdani’s vision is flawed.**_

name: Tim Walz
subject: We can’t let them win.
body:
Peter,
I wanted to emphasize just how serious of a moment we are in.
Donald Trump’s federal agents are going door to door in Minnesota looking for folks based on their accent or color of their skin. Kids are afraid to go to school, small businesses are hurting, another life has been lost, and the people responsible have yet to be held accountable. Instead, Donald Trump’s DOJ is investigating me.
Why? Because my focus has always been protecting the people of Minnesota.
They’re trying to scare me and our state — but Minnesota will not be intimidated into silence and neither will I. This legal battle will be expensive, that’s why they’re doing this. They want me to back down and surrender, but I won’t.
I need your help in this fight. If you’re able, please split a donation between our Legal Defense fund and Outrage then Action and make sure we can continue to stand up to Donald Trump and his DOJ? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp_2026_dga?refcode=partner_ms_tw_em_20260215_realtexaspac]
I'm grateful,
Tim Walz

name: Ted Cruz
subject: Peter, only U.S. citizens should decide U.S. elections
body:
| | | Peter, Secure elections shouldn’t be controversial. American elections should be decided by American citizens, period. **That’s why I’ve championed policies like the SAVE Act, which would strengthen election integrity by requiring proof of citizenship when registering to vote.** **But here’s the reality:** The Left is mobilizing massive resources to oppose election integrity reforms and silence the voices of Americans who want accountability. That’s why Club for Growth is stepping up, building a grassroots movement that supports strong leaders and defends secure elections nationwide. clean_body: | | | Peter, Secure elections shouldn’t be controversial. American elections should be decided by American citizens, period. That’s why I’ve championed policies like the SAVE Act, which would strengthen election integrity by requiring proof of citizenship when registering to vote. But here’s the reality: The Left is mobilizing massive resources to oppose election integrity reforms and silence the voices of Americans who want accountability. That’s why Club for Growth is stepping up, building a grassroots movement that supports strong leaders and defends secure elections nationwide. Your contribution helps Club for Growth: ✅ Expand our grassroots army✅ Amplify the voices of Americans who believe in secure elections✅ Show elected officials nationwide that grassroots conservatives support election integrity reforms. | | | | | | | For liberty, Ted Cruz | | | The Club for Growth is Washington’s premiere conservative organization advocating for policies that lead to economic growth and prosperity for all Americans. Our network includes more than 500,000 pro-growth, limited-government Americans who share the belief that prosperity and opportunity come through economic freedom. \- Reducing the size and scope of government \- Reducing income tax rates and replacing the current taxes with a flat tax \- Passing a balanced budget amendment \- Regulatory reform and deregulation \- Expanding school freedom The Club for Growth will take on any member of Congress’ policies, regardless of their political affiliation. If it stifles economic freedom, we’re there fighting back. Club for Growth 2001 L St. NW, Ste 600 Washington, DC 20036ph 202.955.5500

name: Alex Avetoom, Lincoln Media Foundation
subject: Legacy media hate us
body:
We exist not only to expose that system, but to disrupt it and ensure the full truth reaches the eyes and ears of the American people.

| | |
---
| |
---
| | Peter, Americans are finally coming to the same realization: It's not just that the media has a bias; it's that the media is deciding which stories deserve **attention **-- and which ones _quietly_ disappear. _You 've seen it happen time and again._ A major story breaks, but coverage is soft, delayed, or _oddly_ restrained. Important details are buried **deep** or even _altered_ if they don't fit the writer's liberal worldview. Stories that challenge powerful institutions get a _fraction_ of the attention given to narratives that fit comfortably inside an approved frame. _That 's not random... _It's a corrupt, gatekeeping system, and the American people are the ones suffering from it. **When gatekeepers decide what the public gets to see, truth becomes selective and democracy dies.** **Lincoln Media Foundation exists not only to expose that system, but to disrupt it and ensure the full truth reaches the eyes and ears of the American people.**

clean_body: We exist not only to expose that system, but to disrupt it and ensure the full truth reaches the eyes and ears of the American people. | | | | | | | Peter, Americans are finally coming to the same realization: It's not just that the media has a bias; it's that the media is deciding which stories deserve attention -- and which ones quietly disappear. You 've seen it happen time and again. A major story breaks, but coverage is soft, delayed, or oddly restrained. Important details are buried deep or even altered if they don't fit the writer's liberal worldview. Stories that challenge powerful institutions get a fraction of the attention given to narratives that fit comfortably inside an approved frame. That 's not random... It's a corrupt, gatekeeping system, and the American people are the ones suffering from it. When gatekeepers decide what the public gets to see, truth becomes selective and democracy dies. Lincoln Media Foundation exists not only to expose that system, but to disrupt it and ensure the full truth reaches the eyes and ears of the American people. | | | | As a 501(c)(3) nonprofit media organization, Lincoln Media Foundation was built to expose media bias and fund, produce, and amplify independent journalism, particularly in the states and communities where information directly shapes the direction of the country. We operate a growing digital media network, partner with hundreds of citizen journalists, and distribute content across multiple platforms, including national English-language channels and Spanish-language outreach through La Pura Neta. But just as the truth disrupts the agenda of the mainstream media, so does our existence. That's why we depend so heavily on generous donors like you -- who are sick of media bias & censorship and care about the truth -- to support us and our important work. | | | | Right now, we're active in Wisconsin -- a state whose electoral outcomes affect far more than its own borders. We've helped elevate citizen-driven reporting in Minnesota that legacy outlets are suppressing. Our documentary - which Gavin Newsom and LA Mayor Karen Bass don't want you to see - exposes the ugly truth behind the Pacific Palisades Fire and has received over half a million views and counting. And across our growing digital network -- including national platforms and Spanish-language outreach -- we're reaching audiences that traditional media either ignores, misunderstands, or even deplores. We don't just publish stories; we make sure people actually know the truth. Lincoln Media Foundation was created to cover what others cover up, and to expose the gaps, distortions, and blind spots that shape public perception. But this work doesn't sustain itself. We are not backed by corporate conglomerates or billion-dollar endowments. We rely on individuals who are tired of watching stories disappear and who believe the public deserves access to information without institutional filtering. | | | | If you have felt the frustration of selective coverage. If you have questioned why certain narratives dominate while others barely surface. If you believe reporting truth should not depend on whether it is politically convenient... The alternative is simple but scary: fewer unbiased voices, more centralized control, and a public ignorant of the truth. That is not a future we should accept. Alex Avetoom Lincoln Media Foundation | | | | Lincoln Media Foundation is a California 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation. PO Box 8095, Newport Beach, CA 92658-8095 | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- |

name: Team Kelly
subject: Mark’s story — (PHOTOS)
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
#

Growing up, this little boy learned that through hard work, he could do anything he set his mind to.
[](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-feb26.6dv2ri&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=1&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)
At the age of 23, he became a United States naval aviator. He deployed twice on the U.S.S. Midway and flew 39 combat missions in Operation Desert Storm.
[](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-feb26.6dv2ri&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=2&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)
Then, at the age of 32, he was selected as an astronaut (with his twin brother, Scott!). He flew his first of four missions into space in 2001 aboard Space Shuttle Endeavour. He spent more than 50 days in space and has lived on the International Space Station.
[](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-feb26.6dv2ri&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=3&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)
In 2011, Mark's wife, Former Congresswoman Gabby Giffords, was shot in Tucson during an event where she was meeting with her constituents. Mark retired from the Navy and NASA, and his mission turned from service to his country to caring for his wife. But Gabby's service in Congress, using policy to improve people's lives, stuck with him.
So, in 2020, Mark took on his next mission — running for U.S. Senate in Arizona — and won.
[](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-feb26.6dv2ri&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=4&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)
Then, in 2022, he had to turn around and run for a full term in the Senate. And thanks to our incredible team, he won again.
[](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-feb26.6dv2ri&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=5&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)
Now, Mark’s in yet another important position. As Vice Chair of the Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee, it’s on Mark to find a way to defend every incumbent Democrat in the Senate. And, to win back our majority, he’s got to take four seats back from the GOP, too.
Alongside everything else Mark’s gone through over the past several months, it’s a tall task. But if anybody can do it, it’s him — and he needs your help.
[**Can you make a $3 contribution as we approach a critical end-of-month deadline? Mark can’t do this alone.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-feb26.6dv2ri&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=6&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Donate $3](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1396?c0=mek-em-feb26.uW_fva&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=25&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)**
---
****
| **[Donate $25](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1397?c0=mek-em-feb26.Oy0sNO&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=26&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $50](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1390?c0=mek-em-feb26.9bxNOL&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=27&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)**
---
****
| **[Donate $100](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1391?c0=mek-em-feb26.hGe9Zy&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=28&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)**
---
****
| | **[Donate $250](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1392?c0=mek-em-feb26.OuYjqr&refcode=em260222-1-90&t=29&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I)**
---

---
****
Thank you for being on Mark's team,
Team Kelly

Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=31&akid=6372%2E6688874%2Ewo4t3I).
Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and astronaut. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or Department of Defense and reference to NASA does not imply endorsement by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

name: James O'Keefe
subject: They're Suing Me Because the Cameras Are Working
body:
A Pentagon contractor. A DOJ official. A State Farm VP. All caught on hidden camera. All lawyered up. I need your help to fight back.

| | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z5dfjm0rtf00i5r00anmokgcoknjprlp32pd8290_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)
---
| Dear Friend,
Right now, on my desk, there are three active lawsuits. Not because I broke the law. Not because I lied. **Because I pointed a camera at the wrong people -- and they talked.** A former FBI agent who was working as a Pentagon contractor [**told our undercover journalist**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zmhohntsjos4scu08m6gjj401jakdngck2651dso_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs) he was coordinating with retired generals to "explore what we can do" to "protect people from Trump." He got fired from Booz Allen the day we published. Now he's suing me. A DOJ official sat across from our reporter on what he thought was a date -- and admitted on camera that the government would redact Republican names from the Epstein files while leaving Democrats exposed. The DOJ scrambled to do damage control within hours. Now they want me silenced. A State Farm vice president bragged about how the company orchestrates rate hikes and mocked wildfire victims -- on hidden camera. He was terminated immediately. Now he's filed criminal eavesdropping charges against my reporter in Cook County, Illinois. Let me be direct with you: **_They don't sue you when you're wrong. They sue you when you're right and they can't make the tape go away._** I just released a short video that shows the full scope of what we're up against -- and why we keep going. [**Watch it here:**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z8h72lrtmgh1jcm76g0rl15of130ob4nq40rk44o_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs) [****](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z69b5augk2gn72a7uglfdoc7tbvp4a60hek2gvu0_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs) In that video, you'll hear things that would stop most people cold. Board members at my former organization -- the one I built from nothing -- conspiring to destroy me. Lawyers I personally hired admitting on tape they wanted to assault me. Deposition testimony revealing that the very board members who fired me were co-investing in pharmaceutical companies that "could be sold to big Pharma, like Pfizer" -- while we were actively investigating Pfizer. And still, through all of that, we rebuilt. O'Keefe Media Group launched the day I walked out the door. And in two years, we have done more damage to the establishment than they ever thought possible. **That's why the lawsuits keep coming.** Here's what you need to understand: every one of these legal attacks is designed to do one thing -- make the cost of truth so high that we stop pursuing it. That's the playbook. That's always been the playbook. Bury the journalist in legal fees until the cameras go dark. **I will never let that happen.** But I cannot do this alone. Liberty Guard has established a legal defense fund specifically to keep us in the fight. Every dollar goes directly toward courtroom defense, investigative operations, and protecting the reporters who risk everything to bring you the truth. **Here's what your contribution makes possible:** **[**$25**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zgn7kfdupil8pu1mr5g1vtjdbckgmchner7j3g0g_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)** -- Helps cover court filing costs and legal document preparation **[**$50**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zq4di7nnv6tjugbnshe3fptrlaahggrohtnb29f0_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)** -- Funds one hour of legal research for our active cases **[**$100**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zkq7sksm7nep66e5l1djf4os8f893h43m52120pg_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)** -- Keeps an undercover investigation running for one additional day **[**$250**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2znah0p3bjb0li9s3d7bf6r5dcqos7kltefceura0_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)** -- Covers a portion of deposition preparation against one of our three active lawsuits **[**$500**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z5n9d7cafovob6j1cakh20qcstiuh1v0jpats6h0_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)** -- Directly funds First Amendment defense motions being filed right now **[**$1,000+**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2ze5cht4rhl3f4ns0ggbthq2apk8ngd38csmmau2o_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)** -- Ensures our anti-SLAPP filing in D.C. -- a case that could set nationwide precedent for undercover journalism -- gets the resources it needs to win | | [DONATE NOW -- STAND WITH JAMES](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zti71n6v6cqmft5vpi0gsudga3muquupdr5g42e0_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)
---

The people we've exposed had power, access, and the protection of massive institutions. What they didn't have was the truth on their side.
**We do.**
And with your help, no amount of legal intimidation will silence us.
_My price is my life -- not money, not comfort._
[**Stand with me.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zsf3g5hq3imh2pjdkf6s8kl1c6p4ihqq8q2u4h5g_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)
In the fight,
James O'Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group

**P.S.** The attorney behind the Pentagon contractor's lawsuit has publicly stated he is recruiting additional plaintiffs and planning more lawsuits against our organization. This is a coordinated legal campaign to shut down undercover journalism in America. [**Your donation today doesn't just defend me -- it defends the right of every citizen journalist to hold the powerful accountable.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zhej77jmm1ca7hm9keafem63nluoujtvujs21jto_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)

Pop Acta Media | American Action News
---
_PO Box 1177 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z8i0hcn1k2s0a7t7ufvvk1gg82opdigu3b0u9d8g_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf9tge5rrasbjkgqracjkmoq37d1m65qbfctjmkrncst66uqrf9bjmpqn4djm69qb4d9nkvijbo9getqb3cdkmppmeo0n6mq39cdjkorrbcp4mni6aor3c5h64oh1k8gn48n24hjmep37clge9pj4s8iv0)_ | _[manage data preferences](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zeengvjjjej1rcvriumrv7ajovdjl1tg68r4amp8_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh2bs)_

[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z0aj2adl2n6edk76m8tmoadkfol3712nu0qsjud8_rp22sh2s8i6epr4ctim1p76cjh238h5e8he49h666qbcdli6uon49fg)

name: Parler Sponsor
subject: Gold is Resetting as Money — and NatGold Is Where Capital Is Moving Next
body:
https://click.parlermailer.com/?
View in browser
A Special Message from our SponsorPlease note that views expressed in this message do not necessarily reflect those of Parler and do not constitute an endorsement or recommendation by Parler.
78x Market Reach
‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌

Bitcoin proved something important.

It showed the world that digital scarcity matters — that people are willing to hold value outside fiat systems.

But markets don’t stand still.

Today, even Bitcoin’s strongest advocates like Michael Saylor are discovering a quiet truth:
Bitcoin works best when the price is rising.

Gold works in every environment.

And right now, gold has entered a new phase.

At $5,000+ an ounce, gold isn’t rallying.

It’s resetting.

subject: Climate activists sue over EPA's repeal of 'endangerment' finding
body:
A coalition of 17 environmental and health organizations filed a lawsuit Wednesday challenging the EPA’s decision to repeal a 2009 finding that concluded that greenhouse gas emissions are harmful to human health and fall under the EPA’s jurisdiction to regulate them. The finding impacts a variety of emission sources, but the repeal only impacts the [...]...Click to read more | [](http://trk.libertyledger._ZD01In0/c1EsKvkR6eotbCNpm4GUbQ)

# **[Los Angeles pulls PSA urging riders not to poop on city buses](http://trk.libertyledger._g)**
---
The Los Angeles Department of Transportation has pulled public service announcement videos asking riders to refrain from pooping on city buses. The videos were part of their PSA campaign called “If you see something, do something about it.” The campaign featured six different videos urging riders to report disturbing behavior, including smoking, drinking, fare evasion, [...]...Click to read more

name: Royce White for US Senate
subject: The People Are Coming — Time to Act!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Royce White for US Senate
body:
Support Your Values
| | | | |
---
|
---
America First Starts with the People.
Across the country, Americans are demanding leadership that puts their nation, their communities, and their future first.
America First is about restoring a simple but essential principle: government should serve its own citizens before global agendas, political insiders, or unelected power centers. It means secure borders, safe communities, economic opportunity for working families, and leadership that answers to the people -- not international pressure or special interests.
Putting America First means standing firmly against policies that weaken national sovereignty or place global priorities ahead of the needs of American citizens. It means protecting local communities, defending constitutional rights, and ensuring that decisions affecting our future are made by accountable leaders here at home.
Real change doesn't come from slogans. It comes from engaged citizens who:
* Stay informed
* Ask hard questions
* Show up to public meetings, forums and elections
* Show up at school board, county commissioner, and city council meetings

Politics is local. Demand the search for truth. A strong nation depends on strong communities and citizens who refuse to be ignored.
America First is more than a political phrase. It is a commitment to sovereignty over globalism, responsibility over complacency, and a future built on strength, accountability, and pride in our country.
This is why they fear us: The People Are Coming!
---
| [Donate Now](https://5vev59hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TntDvwFSocOaHEV0cy2qC8HRiqRxe5tVrDEyVUae6uqxb90O80qAloIVFMBXd2NfFKuF-f25jeJ6zcK459picdGq66rh3CcyBOwi9mOZp32YlCL5XgXsVE9Js8Sf9gXPZEnmqS8mhQmMXwvIrFDrouZRQijeTtex29uAxYQhmyg=&c=5ZVb1I462zTzYjLN49scdGRFfUjRoT2sdEvL1l0R4K2vD2O7FXtPOA==&ch=mP6F2iMY6d-zBi3y_Vp-YBV4lwO4j6YhKVsa4cA5sM-2DhRsewHOdA==)
---
Stand Up. Speak Out. Stay Engaged.
If you believe in America First, now is the time to act. More and more Americans are realizing just how important it is to stay informed, ask hard questions, and show up. Across this country, people are rising to ensure these principles stand -- sovereignty over globalism, citizens over insiders, and strength over surrender.
Support leaders who answer to the people, not the political class. Demand accountability. Expect results. Refuse to be ignored.
Hold the line. Stay engaged. America First starts with us -- and it stands because we stand.
In Liberty, Royce White U.S. Senate 2026
---
| |
---
| |
---
| Step In. Support the Fight. If you believe America comes first -- now is the time to show it.
Every dollar helps push back against the insiders, amplify the message, and build a movement strong enough to restore accountability and put Americans first again.
Don't wait. Don't assume someone else will do it. Invest in the future. Stand for America First.

Across the country, Americans are demanding leadership that puts their nation, their communities, and their future first.
America First is about restoring a simple but essential principle: government should serve its own citizens before global agendas, political insiders, or unelected power centers. It means secure borders, safe communities, economic opportunity for working families, and leadership that answers to the people -- not international pressure or special interests.
Putting America First means standing firmly against policies that weaken national sovereignty or place global priorities ahead of the needs of American citizens. It means protecting local communities, defending constitutional rights, and ensuring that decisions affecting our future are made by accountable leaders here at home.
Real change doesn't come from slogans. It comes from engaged citizens who:
* Stay informed
* Ask hard questions
* Show up to public meetings, forums and elections
* Show up at school board, county commissioner, and city council meetings

Politics is local. Demand the search for truth. A strong nation depends on strong communities and citizens who refuse to be ignored.
America First is more than a political phrase. It is a commitment to sovereignty over globalism, responsibility over complacency, and a future built on strength, accountability, and pride in our country.
This is why they fear us: The People Are Coming!
---

---
Stand Up. Speak Out. Stay Engaged.
If you believe in America First, now is the time to act. More and more Americans are realizing just how important it is to stay informed, ask hard questions, and show up. Across this country, people are rising to ensure these principles stand -- sovereignty over globalism, citizens over insiders, and strength over surrender.
Support leaders who answer to the people, not the political class. Demand accountability. Expect results. Refuse to be ignored.
Hold the line. Stay engaged. America First starts with us -- and it stands because we stand.
In Liberty, Royce White U.S. Senate 2026

name: American Action News
subject: Thomas Sowell Trickle Down Theory and Tax Cuts for the Rich
body:
I hear from people all the time that Thomas Sowell is one of their heroes.

| | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2ze47ekooqo3tlimp5m9li6op3cfbh9s487e1j4s8_rp22sh2s8i6epr4cthm6p30c3h2bs)
---
| | Dear Friend,I hear from people all the time that Thomas Sowell is one of their heroes. For those people--and you may be among them--I'm thrilled to share a digital copy of one of his commentaries with you, absolutely free: _[**_"_****_Trickle Down " Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich._****_"_**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zknnhce5aff3qp398g7qhdvpe399bgkp9916t63g_rp22sh2s8i6epr4cthm6p30c3h2bs)_
---
| [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zoqrv7suuhs56gtregsedcid8brkg1l9uv7tkdc0_rp22sh2s8i6epr4cthm6p30c3h2bs)
---
| | "Tax cuts for the rich" is a favorite argument of those who oppose lower tax rates. But does imposing higher tax rates on wealthy Americans actually increase the amount of revenue coming into the federal government? Hoover Institution Senior Fellow **Thomas Sowell takes an empirical approach** in his commentary, [**_"_****_Trickle Down " Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich._****_"_**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zlqmjnql5d1kfblqd2ph0h7mdi9bnvqpjh5je72g_rp22sh2s8i6epr4cthm6p30c3h2bs) Sowell separates fact from fiction when it comes to tax rates and uses real life data and examples to prove that lower tax rates for all actually lead to greater revenue for the government. As a friend and supporter of the Hoover Institution, we are proud to offer you this valuable, thought-provoking resource free of charge.[**Click here to get your free copy of**** _ "Trickle Down" Theory and "Tax Cuts for the Rich" _****now.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zn1nc2o9720qjgio1jbovtv7uc8n963r7q2ptc2o_rp22sh2s8i6epr4cthm6p30c3h2bs) Thanks,Greg Greg Stamps | _Online Development_ Hoover Institution | Stanford University
---
_With its eminent scholars and world-renowned Library and Archives, the Hoover Institution is a non-partisan, data-driven policy research organization that seeks to improve the human condition by advancing ideas that promote economic prosperity, national security, and democratic governance. Over more than one hundred years, Hoover's work has directly led to policies that have produced greater freedom, democracy, and opportunity in the United States and around the world._

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | | I laid eyes on this and IMMEDIATELY put it in my wife’s purse. ## Winter Storm to Blanket East Coast
A nor’easter is expected to bring [significant snow to the East Coast](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFuwyAURb8Gb0TwsI0zMFSqvKVLVXW0MDwSWgwRPMXN31fuL3Q-0tG95_OyXGxMMV_f7IaGQlky7i0hEdYll4q2EVb8uaMj9Cs2amRpAQEjF8ABFm9jei59501ABFQdGqm11MM0qLG7Gb36s5MBFAaQDrxGfT5rxGHSE7jRd9EcNgEAUoppGE7Whkm7fhUuTFJJx3qBPlKpp68SM90wpGd5YD25snXJ3IjujakXBjOD2a7ueHAwBvPHO4O5F3yLKcWSud2wRmdz4zZhJb7HTFh5o1I37kr2kWLJje-I35g9g_lAT6bm6Jl6lUqoUYxCdtX4-35I26hZL66bjelvD_0r4sPAbwAAAP__ZL2G9w) today into Monday, placing New York City under a blizzard warning for the first time in nearly a decade. More than 40 million Americans are on alert for winter storm conditions that could bring more than one foot of snow and gusty winds that will likely cause blizzard conditions as well as coastal flooding. Forecast models suggest [more than two feet of snow](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2O5CAQhU9jZ1iAfzcg2KSz3nRDC0NhaoXBgtqmu08_8ozmBJNV8NXTe9_f-3rXGDDuf_QBilxaI9QSgAjyGlMGXQgyPE8wBHaDQoU0rZLLiXHJpFytxvBah9YqByChb0GJeRbzuIz91HplFit_wWKkMM6IQUz97Oy82cmNBvi0taiuNC6lFIIv49hp7ZbZDBs3bhG9MM3AwSKl3P1LGMmDC6_0gNyZdLRBeaKzNP3vRt4aeau1di49K2jyX0gjbwEfwK5h3_f_02qCwjwWShkN2wK-3zpbRjrvQIUdGAKmWBhGRh5YTJn8ZYNVJH_x0WLcWYmpMowEsQCrGG1ps7Jnvd7LNDcD3w-N4bMr_UjwQ8mPAAAA__-3YJYa) for parts of New Jersey, while the broader I-95 corridor from Philadelphia to Boston is bracing for 12-24 inches. Widespread and prolonged power outages are considered a high probability. There is a possibility that [the storm will strengthen rapidly](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2y2yAQhZ8GdWhg9ZuCIo06p02pWcNikSDwwNrEb59xcue-wK2_M9-cc35e9guGGNLtB55k2Oc9UauRmKnsKRfCylToz50sk7tS5crIOyiYpQIJsDsM8bWPnTOeCGjoyOhl0cu0TsPcHUbreVnUDN7ab55GGq1ylnCd3egtWtcF87YpANBardPUI_p1seNVWb_qQVsxKnKBc-l_5ZD4IB9f-Umlt_nsojmY71UM3wVsArbWWo_WPhohH_8zAjZKbxQSU5EfRMD2uU_yUQhZxpzPKrHKmnKrnMspK7HkLK8R029ieQYnkSMmDlbW_HibkkzUJKVbxOQEbHqdp0Gprhh3byHGUOdFjOp2Yoj_OvOXjn4a-BsAAP__0SSXDg) as it delivers snow to the Northeast for the fifth weekend in a row.
## Appeals Court Allows Display of Ten Commandments
A U.S. appeals court has cleared the way for a Louisiana law requiring poster-sized [displays of the Ten Commandments](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1u7CAQhZ8Gd1gwxj8NxW3c7W1TWmMY7xJhcGB2nX37yHmFdEc60tH5vo_bcsMQQ7r_x50sb3lJdNZIzFSWlAthZSr0fZBj8itVroy8gIJBKpAAi8cQ34tpvN2IgLqGrB5HPfZT3w3Nw5pBr157R8Yr43rj1g3c1PleD16PvW-CvdYUAGitpr5vEbdpdGZVbpt0p50winzgXNrPHBI_aIvv_KLSurw30T6Yjyq6fwJmAfN5nq1b60Vx9QLmKwqYY36GGjChjHjKQl_PUEK6S6YkXd53TH6nxFW6iLWWnPcq8TgIY5UuPwsLmJti_XGGGEMdRmHUfccQf2_wn9y9LPwEAAD__0R8hyU) in public classrooms to take effect. The 5th Circuit Court of Appeals voted 12-6 to lift a block that a lower court first placed on the law in 2024. In the opinion released Friday, the court said it was too early to make a judgment call on the constitutionality of the law. Critics argue the requirement violates the separation of church and state, while supporters say the Ten Commandments are [historical and foundational](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O3SAQRp_G7rAA_96CIo27TZvSGmBYE2GwmPF6_fbRzSts_Umfzjl_PrYPiCnmz99woOFQtow3JWTGuuVSEYix4veJjtFbJCYG3rTUk5BaaL15iOnZhtabgKixb9GoeVbzuIz91O7GDsOi5OxnH2DqX-Gl-sW-rLIowbpxbKN5v0mttVJyGccOICyzG6x0YVG9cs0g0UcutftbYuYdQ3rKF9bOlaNNZmc-qel_NXpt9HrfdxfK99vivTd6vajRa0CPFZJw5aosXEKoJG54BGMWrhwHZH9gZhI-0pngQS9SuSJFyCDOy6boBLm9lCRcAqJaykFtNf68Y0qRprkZ5OcBMf3H4h-1_DL6XwAAAP__92yNjw) to U.S. law. In a concurring opinion, Circuit Judge James Ho wrote, _"It is fully consistent with the Constitution, and what’s more, it reinforces our Founders’ firm belief that the children of America should be educated about the religious foundations and traditions of our country."_
## List: Top Barbecue Joints in the Nation
Franklin Barbecue in Austin, TX tops a list of [best barbecue joints](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0c2O4yAMB_CnITcqMAGSQw4jjXKbvc4x4sO07BCogDbbt1-l2jfYo23Z1k__76_ty8QU8_WX2XHpoWwZj5awd6xbLhVN61jxzx1dR2-x9dZN34CBogwowOZNTK9tHPwSEAHFgAvXmms5SaGG2-KU58EywfRsR8u1sghyljwomCUyN8TlvMYAgHM2SXkxJkzajZa5MHHBHRkZ-thLvfwuMfcbhvQqT6wXV_YhLbfe742IDwIrgfU4jsve8jkjsGKmj0ZgDaV4k72vMf_8K08ngbXfkJ4sak216B5I309ozBSfWF_00ehJRlpN_kFPYG2Nfnzw77TamYi1uOiJ-Dxi9rGi6wSUe75bap6lRTfJcVIjcDFpKSdtJVMyQJCKgMJIxOc4EhBxN1c8l_RQF38_YkqxKU1Gdt1NTG9r_6-Angv8DQAA__-vq6G8) in the United States in a recent ranking based on user reviews, awards, and accolades. Customers at [Franklin Barbecue](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykjz1uMyEUAE8DnS14sLt8BcXXuHPalIifh02CYQNPdnz7yLlC6tGMNO9nd_allnZ58ze0lLtr-JgViXC41gf6STjwe8dImAJOmuTJgYD1IOAA4JIv9ek0TzYjAiqOVm6b3BazqJVfbdSrDFqB8vpfhJiDirj4kIIy2oikebGvmgAAKYVZlqP32WxRBxGzkUpGpgWmQn0cP3ppdMVcn_2O4xj7jVd7JdonU_8ZnBic8vDts5YWwteLMzjxYdP-KLWWuW5Mi8vNl_rr0p-G7xZ-AgAA__8oHWps) line up for the restaurant’s beef brisket with pickles, onions, barbecue sauce, and white bread. [Joe’s Kansas City Bar-B-Que](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFyqzAQRb9GdDDSAkIuVLyGzq9NyazFCuTIEiNtTPz3GecXUt-Ze-acj-tyxRBD2v7jgyz7vCQ6ayRmKkvKhbAyFfo-yDGtN6pcGXkBCbqV0AIsK4b4WoZmtZ4IqG_IqmlS02jGXje7VVoPRl3g4sapp8uqtIeb6wGNAu3INMG-3yQAKCXNOHaI3kxuuEnnjeqVE4OkNXAu3T2HxDv5-MpPKp3Ljybanfmoov8nYBYwn-fZ3TPVT_eeBcwHblQFzLxTm0vYQsLYbljbt0jIqS1UGb8KJm6KXY8zxBiqnsQgtweG-EvhP6V5WvgJAAD__2lCeZw)—known for its toasted Kaiser roll stuffed with slow-smoked beef brisket, provolone, and onion rings—finished second. Lewis Barbecue (Charleston and Greenville, SC), Little Miss BBQ (Phoenix, AZ), and Tyler's Barbeque (Amarillo, TX) round out the top five.

**Whatever happened to ABC's _Wide World of Sports_?** ABC’s _Wide World of Sports_ arrived on Saturday afternoons like a postcard from a bigger planet. [Premiering on April 29, 1961](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFuwyAUgOHT4C0WPBtDB4ZUqodKacdIXSwMj5iKmAhe4vr2VXqFzr_0Df_5NJ1sTHG9fNgrGgp5WnGrCYmwTGsuaCthwZ8bOkI_Y6VKlibgMBw4HAAmb2Pap77xJiACdg0aoZRQUstuaBaj_dx7aXvHpZyDHrQYAkqlw9C9wCyxieapcQAQgmspW2uDVq6fuQtadMKxnqOPlEv7neNKC4a05weW1uVrk8xCdKusOzIYGYzbtrV7vtN9xmdnMNYlF6oMxtevM3y-FfWejk0x_rbFlGIdFOv55Wpj-vPoXxMeBn4DAAD__2g_cOA), the anthology format let ABC hopscotch from boxing to skiing to track—events most Americans rarely saw on television. The concept is often credited to programmer Edgar Scherick, but the show’s signature feel, the cinematic storytelling, and the sense of globe-trotting spectacle were shaped by a young producer named Roone Arledge. Anchoring it all was Jim McKay, steady as a newsman and curious as a traveler. In its early decades, _Wide World_ became more than a time slot. It became a national ritual. The opening tease, _“Spanning the globe...,”_ and the immortal tagline, _“the thrill of victory... and the agony of defeat_ ,” turned sports into weekly drama. You can see [the opening credits from 1969 here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAUheGnwVssuMbYGRgqVdnStSMCfIlpsbHgJjRvX6Wv0PUc6Rv-z6u52pjifvuwG2oK2ezYakIiLGbPBW0lLPhzoCdcHFaqZMkAB3XicAIwi43paWS36IAIOHSoxTSJaZzHQXWrdtadvUTgHkCNSkkhZziHMAlAB4hd1C-NA4AQfB7H3towT1467sMsBuGZ5LhEyqX_ynGnFUN65geW3uetS3olOiob3hhcGFxaa_0z3-nu8PW_Fkt-ZcPlwYb38ylu7rip9i27opejxZRiVROT_LbZmP5I-leHh4bfAAAA__-TOXG3). Then the world changed. Cable arrived, and sports stopped being scarce. ESPN and other networks offered niche events around the clock, while premium rights consolidated into fewer, bigger packages. The very thing that made _Wide World_ essential, its variety, became easier to find everywhere else. Add rising rights costs and shifting network priorities, and the anthology started to feel like a relic of a pre-cable era. The last original episode aired on June 21, 1997, and the show was canceled in early 1998, ending a 37-year run. What replaced it wasn’t a single show, but a new model: ESPN-driven, event-based programming, and eventually the ESPN-on-ABC identity.

In the end, _Wide World of Sports_ didn’t so much collapse as get unbundled, absorbed, and rebranded by the sports media machine it helped invent. _Were you a fan of "Wide World of Sports"? Did you look forward to it each week? Let us know your thoughts by replying to this email._

clean_body: A nor’easter is expected to bring today into Monday, placing New York City under a blizzard warning for the first time in nearly a decade. | | | | | | Sunday, February 22, 2026 ---|--- Good Morning! On this day in** 1980, the U.S. Olympic hockey team stunned the world by beating the four-time defending gold medal-winning Soviet team, 4-3, at the Winter Games in Lake Placid, New York. This unforgettable moment is known as the “.” For decades, ABC’s Wide World of Sports took us around the globe each Saturday afternoon to expand our sense of athletic competition with unique travelogue programming, as seen in today's In case you missed our breakdown of the best and worst states to retire in, tune in to The Flyover Podcast, where host Ayla Brown walks through the new ranking. You won’t be able to guess which state landed at the No.1 spot. ! Today’s sponsor, , is a vertically integrated Colombian coffee producer behind the North American relaunch of Juan Valdez®, giving investors a chance to get in ahead of its planned 2027 IPO. | Winter Storm to Blanket East Coast A nor’easter is expected to bring today into Monday, placing New York City under a blizzard warning for the first time in nearly a decade. More than 40 million Americans are on alert for winter storm conditions that could bring more than one foot of snow and gusty winds that will likely cause blizzard conditions as well as coastal flooding. Forecast models suggest for parts of New Jersey, while the broader I-95 corridor from Philadelphia to Boston is bracing for 12-24 inches. Widespread and prolonged power outages are considered a high probability. There is a possibility that as it delivers snow to the Northeast for the fifth weekend in a row. Appeals Court Allows Display of Ten Commandments A U.S. appeals court has cleared the way for a Louisiana law requiring poster-sized in public classrooms to take effect. The 5th Circuit Court of Appeals voted 12-6 to lift a block that a lower court first placed on the law in 2024. In the opinion released Friday, the court said it was too early to make a judgment call on the constitutionality of the law. Critics argue the requirement violates the separation of church and state, while supporters say the Ten Commandments are to U.S. law. In a concurring opinion, Circuit Judge James Ho wrote, "It is fully consistent with the Constitution, and what’s more, it reinforces our Founders’ firm belief that the children of America should be educated about the religious foundations and traditions of our country." List: Top Barbecue Joints in the Nation Franklin Barbecue in Austin, TX tops a list of in the United States in a recent ranking based on user reviews, awards, and accolades. Customers at line up for the restaurant’s beef brisket with pickles, onions, barbecue sauce, and white bread. —known for its toasted Kaiser roll stuffed with slow-smoked beef brisket, provolone, and onion rings—finished second. () Flying together with our sponsor ➤****Recent reports claim a chemical found in many popular toothpaste brands may create a toxic foam that irritates gums, causing redness, pain, and potential dental problems. Millions of products have reportedly been recalled. to see if you could be affected—and discover a simple household ingredient that may help soothe inflammation and bleeding. | Whatever happened to ABC's Wide World of Sports? ABC’s Wide World of Sports arrived on Saturday afternoons like a postcard from a bigger planet. , the anthology format let ABC hopscotch from boxing to skiing to track—events most Americans rarely saw on television. The concept is often credited to programmer Edgar Scherick, but the show’s signature feel, the cinematic storytelling, and the sense of globe-trotting spectacle were shaped by a young producer named Roone Arledge. Anchoring it all was Jim McKay, steady as a newsman and curious as a traveler. In its early decades, Wide World became more than a time slot. It became a national ritual. The opening tease, “Spanning the globe...,” and the immortal tagline, “the thrill of victory... and the agony of defeat ,” turned sports into weekly drama. You can see . Then the world changed. Cable arrived, and sports stopped being scarce. ESPN and other networks offered niche events around the clock, while premium rights consolidated into fewer, bigger packages. The very thing that made Wide World essential, its variety, became easier to find everywhere else. Add rising rights costs and shifting network priorities, and the anthology started to feel like a relic of a pre-cable era. The last original episode aired on June 21, 1997, and the show was canceled in early 1998, ending a 37-year run. What replaced it wasn’t a single show, but a new model: ESPN-driven, event-based programming, and eventually the ESPN-on-ABC identity. In the end, Wide World of Sports didn’t so much collapse as get unbundled, absorbed, and rebranded by the sports media machine it helped invent. Were you a fan of "Wide World of Sports"? Did you look forward to it each week? Shawn grew up on a Georgia farm. He enlisted after high school. He led troops in combat, served during the Ebola crisis, and spent a lifetime carrying responsibility for others.
When that chapter ended, he didn't chase television cameras -- he came home to raise cattle, support his community, and listen to his neighbors.
What he hears every day is familiar across America: veterans struggling to get care, families squeezed by rising costs, rural communities feeling ignored, and people who work hard but don't feel like anyone in Washington is working for them.
Shawn is running to change that and to bring steadiness, decency, and real problem-solving back to Congress.
Special elections are different. They move fast, and the side that prepares early almost always wins. That's why I'm asking you to help Shawn Harris today.
[If you believe Congress should be a place for serious people doing serious work -- and that leadership should look like service, not performance -- please consider making a contribution right now. Your support will help Shawn reach voters, organize across Northwest Georgia, and build the kind of coalition that can win this race.](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wgEwkuSlrRBfWcIuov7QQ3riuo1_C05p2IuG70nXx11r2aeHNkoNMCqmTTCRQE_fXrMZP6SEt9HEmFycNuxjom3A7dfC5auBcRLXAcY_r_LkBxMaNRg-fjkKa9BFxA98DbCwV1lfM_SKIPYEDdaZrPcZh_ks-Y1yBoEz6giuLXVaD110bbhEU5sKFnr6an_2RREME4EAjEStXtOFBTRnOqt3Dw9mefI-fM8tFKDBafIpAWEMit7_-9Mk15wqZyW45pyrw_xUfYc=&c=vCklW-YOAuauzA4eL-UYyiyTYBuBqUfUY_2OmPoClNviXQRySAj8fg==&ch=gmfH-UPaLq_HWRdOBvX-LV-62bbK7AH-1JedQjr6SAunt5-6HXuUjg==)

clean_body: | | | | If you believe you've received this email in error, you may unsubscribe below. | | | | | | | | == | Friend, I want to tell you about a race in Georgia that deserves your attention. Shawn Harris is running for Congress in Northwest Georgia. He's a retired Army Brigadier General, a Marine veteran, a farmer, and someone who came home after decades of service because he believes public service doesn't end when you take off the uniform. This district has spent years represented by noise instead of leadership -- by spectacle instead of service. And now, for the first time in a long time, there's an opportunity to change that. == | | == | | == | | == | | == | Shawn grew up on a Georgia farm. He enlisted after high school. He led troops in combat, served during the Ebola crisis, and spent a lifetime carrying responsibility for others. When that chapter ended, he didn't chase television cameras -- he came home to raise cattle, support his community, and listen to his neighbors. What he hears every day is familiar across America: veterans struggling to get care, families squeezed by rising costs, rural communities feeling ignored, and people who work hard but don't feel like anyone in Washington is working for them. Shawn is running to change that and to bring steadiness, decency, and real problem-solving back to Congress. Special elections are different. They move fast, and the side that prepares early almost always wins. That's why I'm asking you to help Shawn Harris today. == | | == | == | == | == Thank you, Pete Buttigieg | | == | SHAWN FOR GEORGIA | P.O. Box 1688 | Cedartown, GA 30125 US | | | | | |

We are facing a structural silver deficit that has lasted five straight years—and with China recently clamping down on exports, the physical supply is vanishing faster than the "paper" tickers can keep up with.

When you combine that deficit with the U.S. government's new $12 billion "Project Vault" stockpile, the math is simple: You cannot print more silver to fill the hole.

This "mispricing" is a rare gift before the next supply shock forces a violent repricing.

Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

## South American Group Linked to Robberies
Law enforcement [officials linked more than 60 Houston-area](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOpDAMBuCngc6j4AFyFBTX0O21VyJvYhJLIRklYZjdpz8xujfY2pL___v_fqwfJEGi-0M7z_W1pTXyWQLXynk1WXbOEp2XWnw6Sk2RnkJ1RYUjKATE1ZKEr7Vv7fyp7NS1PHdad3rs9NC1fsZx0EPfs5noPnBvEYk2xm2y2kyqM63M1zOFiB0qxOnW6V6bgdQvHgwS35tesZWa8q16rvyisoWv9OR8M2lvw-xrfZTm_rvBpcHlPM_bll6X4ro3uBylwSVlR1G-2UJJR_VAF8xQhLcRXE7HAz6P7AJl-eYCVwCMCrw4Dxwt_PcDZSbwaecClbLjKtGB5SIucgaXki1tnu3jlBCkjLrpldtJwrtt_dnGzxn_BQAA__-lmZYW) robberies to the transnational organized crime network South American Theft Group. Officials said the group targets second-story rear windows between 7 and 9 p.m., uses screwdrivers to gain entry, disables cameras, jams Wi-Fi signals, and steals high-value designer items. The group hit the Houston suburb of West University Place in January and stole an estimated $500,000 to $650,000 in designer jewelry and other items. The FBI [said the group](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFu4zAMhp_G3hhIdBI3g4ZbsvXWGw1apiyishRIdNK-_cG9vsGNBH5-wPf9eZ_eSZLk9Tdt7PQzlCnzqyVW5Tr5KhtXyWsUbbHsTUump5BOaPAKBgFxWkjS13TuFzeb5WZ7dnYc7Xi148X20YXB0zLjHNAy2YA39n4gQ_5tGGZ78724A2YQ0aJBvJ3seB79hcwbXzwSD93Z8CJa6kkjK39SC-mrPLmefNn65KLqo3XDrw7vHd41ciaVkikt3D6OTYf3Q6rDOx06nhocN-TyOh4OIpQQxAulBknyB1wN_PgCVSaY97omqsINtEAru0b4gWXQyEFhrWV_gKTEKyWQbZO1UtYG3xX_jfrqlsdLUpJ2HbuzWTeS9K2h_xf_6fBvAAAA___q9aA_) consists of "foreign nationals of South American descent" who are in the U.S. illegally or who have overstayed visas. One man was arrested last month, and the FBI [considers the group](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOpDAMQL8mdBkFL0OGguKadHvtlcgEQ6wNySgxw-7fnzjNH1xty-_5_fmcPpEjp-037jTK95qnRGeNJEJl8oV3Kpy2wFJDPqrkhC9GmcBArw1ogGlBjj9T1yzjbJahbWhsrW1t39p724Sxx6WzHQ4wz37Ax_zoZljXoe_B04AP3_B4HTMA0IIBGG6t7ay_o3nQ3QPSh-oMLSy53CSQ0DfWNf7kF5Wbz3sTxyDyrOrjlwKnwJ3nefOR_Re8ha8tBe56S4GL2WNU4C6mAmdAgWvtNcBTU1pz8bRTEh05fVVd8yFB41XBY9ISaBW9lXw8tWTdGx3yTno-yhaxMFXNSb-5utDGOSlwTRmX58kxcu2t6sy2I8d_8vJ_yV8j_A0AAP__bqyYIQ) a national security concern.
## Report Shows Texas Man Killed by DHS
A U.S. citizen was killed by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent during a late-night traffic stop [last March](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykUDuupDAQPA1kHtmNsSFwsMlks-mGqLHbYMngken57emfmCu8sFQfVdW_23TDlNO-_MWNHL9jmXZ6HZmYqU6-po1q2pc18bGWx8Flx2dCnkCCERIEwBQw5c-k2-BmGUbVklPWKmuU7VW7OunjbGbscRi0p0FqZUDbYIaOTEf92CZ3hkkAUCABxouy2voe5UC9B6Su0ZJC4lIvvBLTG4-YP-VJ9eLL1ma3Mt-PpvvTwLWBK97PASd1gsrJZ2rg-vWJ5Ekcaymc9kXUx0y7qPgR26nb6b8IhLwKo02IEBUoiKiHoDSOM1gZpfZdtKatLtxfKed0GNtouWyY8rcM_-7Cp4OfAAAA___bc4Va), according to a newly released report. According to a report from nonprofit watchdog group American Oversight, Ruben Ray Martinez was shot by an ICE agent on South Padre Island after [refusing to exit](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGPnDAUhH-N6UD2O8BQuEjj7q5JopTI2G_hKQYj--2ym18fsX_hupFGmm9m_nxOn44i7cuX29Dw85amHc8SkRnz5DNtmGlfVuKypnvhtLsHOZ5AQl9LqAGm4Ci-prYKZpZhVBUapbXSvdKdqlbjxlEOWvbz7F2nBz8MrpsD-gB69kMrKzJXmAQABRJgbJRute-cHLDz4PBDtBIDccoNr8j4dOUWX-mBufFpq6JZmY8iPn4IsALseZ7N_aDLE2B_pWP6wrMIsL9_CrAXSoCVcGklwHpi-od7XdbENW0bLdkxpb322fm_IZ17_UbWIKETYJUer3n90GpoBdgqm3CcFCOVXotWLpuj-C7G37vzYeB_AAAA__-n94aZ) his vehicle and attempting to strike the agent with his vehicle. The incident was covered by [local news sources](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOwjAQRL_G6YzsJdikcHFNOq69MlrshazkxMheCPz9CXR_cN0UT28083OaTsiZ1-s3LhTkeSnTSlvLJEJ1ipUXqrxeZ5Y2l3uTsuKDUSYw4LQBDTAl5Pya-i6Fs0mD7ShY76131h9sN4d0jGj73hpn6AwEBgdycTBkHZ4vPnYc3jIDABYMwLCzvvfxgOZIhwhIe9UbSiyl7mQmoSe2S36VB9VdLEuXwyxya2r_pWBUMG7b9uYWrrV8CAXje5KC8d70X_pY9IKrbnMRnQiT5kjausEdHXQ1pNvGOXNzXvXmuiDnT5v876NHgN8AAAD___7Xe8w) but wasn't made public by the Department of Homeland Security. Data from ICE show a 400% spike in use-of-force incidents, while the DHS has reported 23 separate instances where agents were attacked or injured during the Trump administration's immigration enforcement operation.
## Former NFL Player Indicted in Fraud Scheme
Former NFL player and [McKinney native Keith J. Gray](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2SpCAUhZ9GMyy4onQHBJuYzaYbWghXvVUIU3BHu99-y36FyU5wfup8_77mL0eR0vbXHWj5teY54VUjMmOZfaEDC6VtJ657_qmckzvJ8QwSRiFBAMzBUXzPug12keGpWrTKGGVGZQbV7nbUS3jIEZ56NF75xSuUsA4AekEM2Ldk7zIJAAokwLNTRhs_OPnAwYPDvtESA3EuHe_I-HJ1je98Yul8Ptpod-bv2vR_GpgamK7r6tb80veN29DAdMsGpk9URLeIfCUswud0kmcMoofHITZMyOQFY2VKm1iL-wmi-h0PbIsN3xfFSHU0jZbb4Sh-5vl30E4L_wMAAP__9NyDdw) was convicted in a $328 million genetic testing fraud scheme. Gray, who played at UConn before the Carolina Panthers drafted him, paid kickbacks through sham "marketing hours" contracts, targeting Medicare beneficiaries and pressuring doctors into ordering unnecessary tests. Gray [owned and operated](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0DGymzAQgOHTQCdGWoNlFyrS0L20KZm1tILNCImR1vB8-0xyhVzgn3--X1_LF3LivP7EnZx8x7JkuloiEaqLr7xT5bxuLG0r7yYl48koC2i4Kw0KYAnI6bOMfXAvHZ6mJ2esNfZu7GT6zVmyZvLjYwoap9E_ptf0fJHFGMd7NKR7dn9jGgAMaIDnYOxo_YT6QZMHpFs3agospQ6ykdA3tpg-5aQ6-LL3yW0iR-tuPzqYO5iv6xp-v5uwp2EtZwdzObCD-agdzLHUnarKMakj4YeqwhxUwlepKKV-VLkyVeVLPtkLBXWDx65WyiTslVATzquKFd9BNb_RTn114bg4JW5324163ZHTvzH5P87TwZ8AAAD__wonjU8) two clinical laboratories, Axis Professional Labs LLC and Kingdom Health Laboratory LLC. [Gray was convicted](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2SgyAUhZ9GOxy4_mAKim3ssu2WDoGL3oiSAaLx7XfcV9j6nDlzvu_nPt41edqmb72iyh8Xxg2P5DFnjKOJtGKkbZoppzm8Uw6b3knnETh0jAMDGK0mf45NadWD25soUQkpheyEbEU5K2lQ9MgFdlgbLdoGLbhH38kenLO2LUldYxwABHCAWyVkI02reY-tAY110XC0lEOs8owZPzo5f4YdY2XCWno15_xKRf1VwFDAcBxH5cLnorjyAob0CjGnAobnO57MhG0nkxPbnGcLUp7Zk01Rn6yGnq3kPYWNrWjJ6IjMRf22LJkZV2QLmeWhzZLKqOzruLqpk0XDp1WT_7uT_ydxV_AbAAD__yQviho) of conspiracy to defraud the U.S., five counts of violating the anti-kickback statute, and he faces three additional counts of money laundering.
